questions.title: ______ is father of Pashto literature.
questions.explanation:
Khushal Khan Khattak (1613–1689) is widely regarded as the father of Pashto literature due to his vast contributions in poetry, prose, and philosophy.

He promoted nationalism, unity, and resistance through his powerful Pashto poetry, influencing generations of Pashtun thinkers and writers.

questions.title: The 2023 Pakistan census revealed the largest population growth in which province?
questions.explanation:

Based on that growth rate ranking, Sindh experienced the largest population growth among the listed provinces in 2023.

questions.title: A proper definition of Muslim is incorporated in the constitution?
questions.explanation:
The 1973 Constitution of Pakistan is the first to define a "Muslim" officially in legal and constitutional terms.

Article 260 of the 1973 Constitution includes the definition of a Muslim as someone who believes in the Oneness of Allah and the finality of Prophethood of Muhammad (PBUH).

questions.title: In 2024, the highest foreign direct investment (FDI) into Pakistan came from ______?
questions.explanation:
In fiscal year 2024 (FY24), Pakistan received approximately USD 1.9 billion in foreign direct investment (FDI), marking a 17% year-on-year increase from FY23.

China was the largest investor, contributing around USD 568 million—about 30% of the total FDI—followed by Hong Kong (USD 359 m) and the UK (USD 268 m).

questions.title: The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) entered which phase in 2023?
questions.explanation:
In 2023, CPEC entered Phase II, focusing on industrialization, socio-economic development, and agriculture after completing Early Harvest Projects.

This phase emphasizes high-quality growth through special economic zones, industrial parks, and science & technology initiatives.

questions.title: Pakistan faced a major electricity blackout in January 2023 during its _____?
questions.explanation:
On 23 January 2023, a national grid frequency fluctuation caused a massive blackout affecting 220 million people in Pakistan.

The outage was due to faulty grid synchronization during energy-saving shutdowns, leading to unstable frequency (~50.75 Hz) and a 12-hour power collapse.

questions.title: What is the main theme of Pakistan’s updated National Security Policy (2022 to 2026)?
questions.explanation:
Pakistan’s first-ever National Security Policy (NSP) for 2022–2026 places economic security at its core, marking a shift from the traditional focus solely on military strength.

It emphasizes building a strong economy to bolster overall national resilience.

questions.title: What was the status of the Mughal Empire after the death of Aurangzeb Alamgir?
questions.explanation:
During Aurangzeb's reign (1658–1707), the Mughal Empire began its decline due to:
  • Military Overextension: Constant wars (e.g., Deccan campaigns) drained resources.
  • Religious Policies: Aurangzeb’s reversal of Akbar’s tolerance alienated Hindus and Sikhs, sparking rebellions (e.g., Sikh uprisings, Rajput conflicts).
  • Economic Strain: Heavy taxation and famine weakened the agrarian economy.
  • Administrative Breakdown: Corruption grew, and regional governors (like the Nizam of Hyderabad) gained autonomy.

As of February 2025, China contributed 40% of Pakistan’s total FDI in infrastructure during FY 2024–25.

China invested $790 million in sectors like power, energy, and transport, making it the top foreign investor.

questions.title: In February 2025, which international financial institution conducted a Governance and Corruption Diagnostic Assessment in Pakistan?
questions.explanation:
In February 2025, the International Monetary Fund (IMF) conducted a Governance and Corruption Diagnostic Assessment in Pakistan.

The IMF dispatched a mission to Islamabad from February 6-14, 2025, to assess governance and corruption risks in Pakistan at the request of the Pakistani government.

The assessment was part of the Extended Fund Facility (EFF) 2024 program.

questions.title: A plateau is a flat elevated landform that rises sharply above the surrounding area on at least one side; in Pakistan, the number of Plateaus is/are ______?
questions.explanation:
Pakistan has two main plateaus: the Pothohar Plateau in Punjab and the Balochistan Plateau.

Both are elevated landforms with distinct geographical and strategic importance.

questions.title: The total requirement of oil produced by Pakistan is ______?
questions.explanation:
Pakistan produces only about 20% of its total oil requirement.
The remaining 80% is met through imports, making the country heavily dependent on foreign oil.

questions.title: When did General Pervez Musharraf assume control of the government by overthrowing Nawaz Sharif?
questions.explanation:
In October 1999, General Pervez Musharraf led a military coup to overthrow Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif.

The military seized control of the government on October 12, 1999.

By October 14, 1999, Musharraf was acting as the Chief Executive of Pakistan.

questions.title: Name the empire ruled by Genghis Khan?
questions.explanation:
Genghis Khan founded and led the Mongol Empire in the early 13th and 14th centuries.

It was the largest contiguous land empire in history.

Founded: 1206

Date dissolved: 1368
